summary: Iteration 3 backend testing for customizable avatar appearance feature. 11/11 new appearance tests + 37/37 regression tests = 48/48 passing. All review_request bullets covered: auth gate, partial merge, full body, unknown field ignored, /auth/me reflects update, back-fill on PUT for legacy users (no appearance field), /cohab/me partner.appearance included, /friends list items include appearance. DEFAULT_APPEARANCE constant has all 8 expected keys.

minor.issue: Legacy users without an `appearance` field do NOT get back-filled on /auth/me. Back-fill only happens in POST /api/auth/session (Emergent OAuth path) or on first PUT /api/auth/appearance. Frontend that calls /auth/me directly with a pre-existing Mongo user will see no appearance field until either re-login or first PUT. Consider also back-filling in get_current_user / /auth/me for full coverage. Currently tolerated by test (test_legacy_user_me_no_appearance_initially).

action_items: Optional: back-fill DEFAULT_APPEARANCE in GET /api/auth/me for legacy users so that mobile clients that bypass /auth/session (e.g. direct token reuse) always see a populated appearance object. Currently only PUT /auth/appearance or re-session triggers back-fill.
critical_code_review_comments: PUT /auth/appearance correctly uses body.dict(exclude_unset=True) for partial merge and ignores extras due to Pydantic AppearanceUpdate model (no extra='allow').
DEFAULT_APPEARANCE keys exactly match the 8 fields required by the spec.
GET /api/cohab/me and GET /api/friends both return raw user docs (with _id excluded) so partner.appearance and friend.appearance are included automatically — verified.
Minor: server.py is ~700 lines — close to the 700-line split threshold; consider splitting into routers (auth, house, friends, chat, cohab) if growth continues.
Minor: get_current_user does not back-fill appearance; only POST /auth/session does. /auth/me on a legacy user will return user without appearance — could surprise frontend.
seed_data_creation: Test fixture seeds 5 users (A,B,F1,F2 with appearance; L legacy without appearance), an A-B cohabitation, and an F1-F2 friendship directly in Mongo via motor; all cleaned up in module teardown. Emails prefixed TEST_APP_ for easy identification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Appearance feature fully covered in /app/backend/tests/test_appearance_backend.py. Run with EXPO_BACKEND_URL set to the preview host. Regression suites test_peeps_backend.py (17) + test_cohab_backend.py (20) remain green. If main agent adds back-fill in GET /auth/me, update TestAppearanceBackfill::test_legacy_user_me_no_appearance_initially to require appearance present with defaults.
